title: Aureline fallback-confidence record and degraded-build-capability handoff contract
description: Boundary schema for the fallback_confidence_record every consumer (run / test / build / debug picker, quick-open, command palette, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheet, CI overlay, support-bundle exporter) reads when the active build adapter is in a degraded posture: a heuristic or imported guess stands in for a native structured capability, the source-of-truth adapter is missing capability flags, target ids round-trip with a narrower confidence ceiling than the picker normally renders, or the workspace falls back to opaque preservation. Companion to schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json (adapter classes, confidence ceiling, capability flags), schemas/tooling/target_graph_snapshot.schema.json (graph_id, partial_truth_class, freshness_class), schemas/tooling/target_descriptor.schema.json (target_id, four typed *_action_support descriptors, target_health_state), and schemas/tooling/adapter_drift_event.schema.json (adapter drift events that emit these records). Companion narrative: docs/tooling/fallback_confidence_and_adapter_drift_contract.md. The schema also freezes one degraded_capability_handoff_record family every consumer surface renders when a target sits in a degraded posture so the six visible classes inspect_only / rerun_last / external_handoff / known_missing / known_unsupported / currently_inferred remain distinct rather than collapsing into a generic Cannot run label. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw secret values, raw env bodies, raw lockfile contents, and raw stdout / stderr never appear in any payload here; producers retain raw inputs out-of-band and cite opaque refs. Adding a new source_kind_class, missing_capability_class, downgraded_behavior_class, recovery_or_escalation_action_class, degraded_capability_class, delivery_surface_class, or handoff_affordance_class is additive-minor and bumps fallback_confidence_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

fallback_confidence_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the fallback-confidence and degraded-capability-handoff contract. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields and new enum members do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 UTC timestamp captured from a monotonic clock source.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log and export. Raw secrets, raw absolute paths, raw URLs, raw command lines, and raw payload bytes never appear here.

dotted_id.description: Dotted lower-snake id used for adapter_id refs and producer_tool refs.

short_sentence.description: Short reviewer-facing sentence safe to show in pickers, problems pane chips, output pane headers, AI action sheets, and support exports. Raw payload bytes, raw command lines, raw env bodies, raw URLs, raw paths, and raw secret values never appear here.

confidence_class.description: Re-export of schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json#/$defs/confidence_class. Pickers and AI planners MUST NOT raise the confidence tier above the active adapter's adapter_confidence_ceiling on a fallback record.

adapter_class.description: Re-export of schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json#/$defs/adapter_class. Capability loss MUST name the adapter class so a heuristic posture is never confused with a native runner posture.

source_kind_class.description: Closed source-kind vocabulary. Names the inputs the active adapter is consuming when the fallback record is emitted. heuristic_target_inferrer_ruleset, structured_importer_export, support_bundle_replay, import_probe_export, ai_proposed_pending_review, opaque_unsupported_preservation, and no_adapter_attached MUST NOT pair with confidence_tier higher than structured_parse_match (and the more-restricted classes below MUST NOT pair higher than heuristic_best_effort).

missing_capability_class.description: Closed missing-capability vocabulary. Names the capability the active adapter no longer publishes or never published; consumers read this set to narrow affordances rather than rendering a generic not_available state. Mirrors the closed capability_flag vocabulary in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json plus the two confidence-ceiling missing classes which name the loss when an adapter swap takes the workspace off an authoritative or structured ceiling.

downgraded_behavior_class.description: Closed downgraded-behavior vocabulary. Names the visible affordance change a consumer surface MUST apply when this fallback record is rendered. Pickers MUST narrow the action chip to the matching state; problems pane MUST disclose the cause in the freshness chip; output pane header MUST disclose the capability chip; AI action sheets MUST disable the corresponding tool call until the user admits.

recovery_or_escalation_action_class.description: Closed recovery / escalation vocabulary. Every fallback_confidence_record MUST cite at least one recovery or escalation action; safe-by-default actions route through review and never discard current work or session state. no_recovery_admissible_review_only is reserved for capability losses whose only honest action is documenting the loss for support export (for example a fallback to opaque preservation when no other adapter can serve the workspace).

adapter_id_ref.description: Ref to the adapter_descriptor_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) that is currently attached when this record is emitted. Resolves through that schema rather than re-encoding adapter rows here.

publishing_graph_id_ref.description: Ref to the target_graph_snapshot_record this adapter most recently published under the workset_scope; consumers cite this ref so the picker resolves the same (graph_id, target_id) pair across re-renders.

snapshot_partial_truth_class.description: Optional partial-truth class projected from schemas/tooling/target_graph_snapshot.schema.json. Null when no snapshot has been published yet.

current_adapter_snapshot.description: Snapshot of the active adapter at the moment this fallback record was emitted. Pickers, AI planners, and CI overlays read this block instead of guessing which adapter is attached.

workset_scope_ref.description: Ref to the workset_scope object in schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json the record was resolved under. The record does not re-mint scope vocabulary.

confidence_tier.description: Confidence tier projected onto this record. MUST NOT exceed the current_adapter_snapshot.adapter_confidence_ceiling and MUST NOT be higher than structured_parse_match when source_kind_class is structured_importer_export, support_bundle_replay, or import_probe_export, and MUST NOT be higher than heuristic_best_effort when source_kind_class is heuristic_target_inferrer_ruleset, ai_proposed_pending_review, opaque_unsupported_preservation, or no_adapter_attached.

missing_capability_class_set.description: Set of capability flags the current adapter no longer publishes or never published. MUST be non-empty: a fallback record exists precisely because at least one capability is missing relative to the prior ceiling. Mirrors the capability_flag set in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json.

downgraded_behavior_class_set.description: Set of visible affordance changes consumer surfaces MUST apply when this record is rendered. MUST be non-empty: a fallback record without a visible behavior change is a silent capability loss.

recovery_or_escalation_action_class_set.description: Set of recovery / escalation actions consumer surfaces MAY offer. MUST be non-empty so the user is never left with a Cannot run label and no next step. no_recovery_admissible_review_only is the legitimate single-element value for capability losses whose only honest action is documenting the loss in support export.

affected_target_id_refs.description: Refs to target_descriptor_record target_ids the fallback record applies to. Empty when the loss is workspace-wide (for example, no_adapter_attached); non-empty when the loss narrows specific targets.

affected_action_class_set.description: Set of run / test / build / debug actions affected by the fallback. Empty when the loss does not narrow specific actions (for example freshness_demoted_to_stale_pending_refresh applied to all four).

lifecycle_status_card_ref.description: Optional ref to a lifecycle_status_card_record (in schemas/ux/lifecycle_status_card.schema.json) the fallback record fans out to. Pickers and AI action sheets read this to surface the keyboard-reachable inspect path and last-failure summary that already governs degraded UX.

linked_adapter_drift_event_id_ref.description: Optional ref to the adapter_drift_event_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_drift_event.schema.json) that emitted this fallback record.

linked_adapter_replacement_event_id_ref.description: Optional ref to the adapter_replacement_event_record (in schemas/tooling/adapter_descriptor.schema.json) that triggered this fallback record.

support_export_disclosure_class.description: Closed support-export disclosure vocabulary. Pickers, support-bundle exporters, and AI brokers read this to decide how the fallback record renders in a support export. redacted_pending_policy_review is reserved for losses pinned by an active policy review and MUST pair with recovery action escalate_to_workspace_admin_via_policy_review.

fallback_confidence_record.description: One fallback-confidence record. Pickers, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheets, CI overlays, and support-bundle exporters read this record so heuristic / imported / replayed truth never masquerades as native structured capability.

degraded_capability_class.description: Closed degraded-capability vocabulary. The six visible classes inspect_only / rerun_last / external_handoff / known_missing / known_unsupported / currently_inferred MUST remain distinct on every consumer surface; a generic Cannot run label is non-conforming when one of these classes applies.

delivery_surface_class.description: Closed delivery-surface vocabulary. Names the consumer surface a degraded-capability handoff renders on. Every degraded_capability_handoff_record MUST cite at least one delivery surface so the loss is visible somewhere.

handoff_affordance_class.description: Closed affordance vocabulary. Each degraded_capability_class binds to a default affordance set (enforced by the affordance-resolution invariant below) so pickers, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheets, CI overlays, and support exports all render the same loss the same way.

handoff_payload_ref.description: Opaque ref to the retained handoff payload. Raw URLs, raw command lines, raw secret values, and raw paths never appear inline. MUST be non-null when handoff_kind is not no_handoff_admissible.

task_event_envelope_id_ref.description: Ref to the prior task_event_envelope (in schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json) the rerun re-binds to. Raw command lines never appear inline; the envelope's adapter retains the invocation payload out-of-band.

rerun_last_invocation_payload.description: Payload for rerun_last_known_invocation_only. The picker re-binds to the prior task envelope rather than guessing a new invocation.

preserves_current_work.description: MUST be true: recovery actions never discard current work or session state to regain capability.

routes_through_safe_review.description: MUST be true for actions that mutate workspace, extension, policy, or adapter attachment state. open_external_documentation and open_in_provider_*_external_handoff MAY set this to false because they delegate to an external provider rather than mutating local state.

lifecycle_status_card_ref.description: Optional ref to the lifecycle_status_card_record that hosts the recovery affordance, the keyboard-reachable inspect path, and the last-failure reason.

target_id_ref.description: Ref to the target_descriptor_record target_id this handoff narrows. Pickers cite this ref alongside publishing_graph_id_ref so the (graph_id, target_id) pair stays stable across re-renders.

publishing_graph_id_ref.description: Ref to the target_graph_snapshot_record graph_id this handoff resolves under.

affected_action_class.description: Names which of the four actions this handoff narrows. One handoff record per (target_id, action) pair so pickers render four typed chips rather than a single ambiguous label.

delivery_surface_class_set.description: Set of consumer surfaces this handoff renders on. MUST include support_export so the capability loss survives a support bundle export; MUST include at least one in-product surface (run_test_build_debug_picker / quick_open / command_palette / problems_pane_chip / output_pane_header_chip / ai_action_sheet / ci_overlay) so the loss is visible to the user, not only to support.

handoff_affordance_class_set.description: Set of affordances consumer surfaces MUST render. Bound to degraded_capability_class by the affordance-resolution invariant so the six visible classes stay distinct.

external_handoff.description: External-handoff payload when degraded_capability_class is external_handoff_only. MUST be non-null with handoff_kind not equal to no_handoff_admissible in that case; MUST be null otherwise.

rerun_last_invocation.description: Rerun payload when degraded_capability_class is rerun_last_known_invocation_only. MUST be non-null in that case; MUST be null otherwise.

linked_fallback_confidence_record_id_ref.description: Optional ref to the fallback_confidence_record this handoff projects from.

degraded_capability_handoff_record.description: One degraded-capability handoff. Pickers, quick-open, command palette, problems pane, output pane header, AI action sheets, CI overlays, and support-bundle exporters render exactly these affordances when an action is degraded so the six visible classes stay distinct.

allOf.description: structured_importer_export, support_bundle_replay, and import_probe_export source kinds MUST cap confidence_tier at structured_parse_match.
heuristic_target_inferrer_ruleset, ai_proposed_pending_review, opaque_unsupported_preservation, and no_adapter_attached source kinds MUST cap confidence_tier at heuristic_best_effort.
opaque_unsupported_preservation source kind MUST cap confidence_tier at unknown and pair with adapter_class fallback_opaque_preservation.
redacted_pending_policy_review support-export disclosure MUST pair with the escalate_to_workspace_admin_via_policy_review recovery action.
external_handoff_only degraded capability MUST carry a non-null external_handoff payload whose handoff_kind is not no_handoff_admissible.
rerun_last_known_invocation_only degraded capability MUST carry a non-null rerun_last_invocation payload.

Non-external_handoff degraded capabilities MUST set external_handoff to null so an external handoff chip never renders for an inspect-only / rerun-last / known-missing / known-unsupported / currently-inferred class.

Non-rerun-last degraded capabilities MUST set rerun_last_invocation to null so a rerun-last chip never renders for an inspect-only / external-handoff / known-missing / known-unsupported / currently-inferred class.
inspect_only degraded capability MUST carry the render_inspect_only_chip_with_keyboard_inspect_path affordance.
rerun_last_known_invocation_only degraded capability MUST carry the render_rerun_last_known_invocation_chip_with_review affordance.
external_handoff_only degraded capability MUST carry the render_external_handoff_chip_with_provider_open affordance.
known_missing_capability_no_recovery_in_context degraded capability MUST carry the render_known_missing_chip_with_install_or_attach_review affordance.
known_unsupported_capability_in_adapter_class degraded capability MUST carry the render_known_unsupported_chip_with_no_action_admissible affordance.
currently_inferred_heuristic_only degraded capability MUST carry the render_currently_inferred_chip_with_review_required affordance.
currently_inferred_heuristic_only degraded capability MUST cap confidence_tier at heuristic_best_effort.
delivery_surface_class_set MUST include support_export so the capability loss survives a support bundle export.
recovery_guidance MUST set preserves_current_work to true; recovery actions never discard current work to regain capability.
